channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI launches new GPT-4.1 models with improved coding
item.description: OpenAI on Monday launched its new AI model GPT-4.1, along with smaller versions GPT-4.1 mini and GPT-4.1 nano, touting major improvements in coding, instruction following, and long context comprehension.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg Testifies in Antitrust Trial
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg defends Meta against allegations of monopolizing the social media market, potentially forcing the tech giant to break off Instagram and WhatsApp.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia lays out plans to build AI supercomputers in the US
item.description: There was mixed reaction from industry analysts Monday over an announcement from Nvidia that it plans to produce AI supercomputers entirely in the US for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judgment Day is coming… for AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence has great potential in predicting outcomes. While AI can predict the likelihood of precipitation, it most likely won’t help you dress or prepare for inclement weather. That preparation involves more than the probability of rain or snow.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster
item.description: Shadi Azoum, the NAVWAR’s program manager for the SBIR/STTR programs, said about 60% of their entire SBIR and STTR awards take advantage of Catapult. The post NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Can’t securely login to your PC using facial recognition anymore? New Windows 11 update patch is likely to blame – if you can install the update at all
item.description: Windows 11’s new update is breaking facial recognition logins with Windows Hello, and also simply failing to install.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple TV+ confirms it's game over for Mythic Quest as the hit show is canceled after 4 seasons
item.description: Mythic Quest will end after season 4, with one final episode airing next week to give one of the best Apple TV+ shows a good send off.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: Sony says it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lutnick: Smartphone tariff exemptions are temporary
item.description: The Trump administration's move to exempt smartphones, computers and other electronics from sweeping "reciprocal" tariffs is only a temporary measure, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday, indicating those devices would be covered by upcoming sector-based tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: White House record-keeping revamp
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A multicloud experiment in agentic AI: Lessons learned
item.description: Recently I undertook a project to design and validate agentic AI architectures capable of operating autonomously across various public cloud providers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How thieves use new credit card numbers before you receive them
item.description: The CyberGuy provides tips to protect yourself from criminals who use various methods to make unauthorized transactions using your account information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA under review: Trump memo spurs scrutiny and uncertainty
item.description: The Trump administration has issued a directive against Christopher Krebs, the founding director and former head of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) for weaponizing and abusing his government authority during his tenure.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s BigQuery and Looker get agents to simplify analytics tasks
item.description: Google has added new agents to its BigQuery data warehouse and Looker business intelligence platform to help data practitioners automate and simplify analytics tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Drones are the future of cybercrime
item.description: The Ukrainian military is teaching the world how to creatively use drones for battlefield offense and defense. Ukraine has built a vast arsenal of drones customized for precision strikes, reconnaissance, and kamikaze missions. But this innovation also extends to cybersecurity offense and defense.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: April update has broken Windows Hello for some devices but there’s a quick fix
item.description: A software hiccup has occurred with Windows Hello following this month’s security updates, one that could prevent users from logging in via facial recognition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IETF hatching a new way to tame aggressive AI website scraping
item.description: For web publishers, stopping AI bots from scraping their best content while consuming valuable bandwidth must feel somewhere between futile and nigh impossible. It’s like throwing a cup of water at a forest fire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GitHub Advanced Security now offers security campaigns
item.description: GitHub has made security campaigns available for GitHub Advanced Security and GitHub Code Security users. Security campaigns help control security debt and manage risk by enabling collaboration between developers and security teams, GitHub said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Android feature prevents sensitive content in Google Messages
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how Google kept Android SafetyCore details quiet until it scanned users' photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hydrogen fuel cell trucks hit the roads in Georgia
item.description: Hydrogen trucks debut in Georgia, sparking debates on costs and green freight gains. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson comments on this promising chapter in green logistics evolution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
